subjectLine: Your AI stack has a NIS2 problem you haven't filed a report on yet
guardianNotes: Draft is exceptionally well-aligned with CORPUS facts, personas, compliance frameworks, and ZRA philosophy. Two minor issues: one editing artifact ('APIRE named' header fragment) and one scope-clarity issue on closed models. All 27+ threat categories, 5-layer architecture, NIS2/EU AI Act mappings, zero-retention claims, statistics (86%, 67%, $4.2M, 2,500+, в‚¬10M, 160,000+, 8.5%, $670K, 98%, 89%), and persona-targeting language are verified and correctly cited.

sequence.subject: Your AI stack has a NIS2 problem you haven't filed a report on yet
sequence.previewText: 86% of enterprises had AI security incidents last year. Most couldn't prove what happened.
sequence.htmlBody:
86% of enterprises had AI security incidents last year. Most couldn't prove what happened.
APIRE.
AI Security

Your AI deployments are generating security events NIS2 requires you to report.
Most organisations have no record of them.

Last year, 86% of companies experienced AI security incidents. The same organisations are now subject to NIS2 fines of up to в‚¬10 million or 2% of global turnover вЂ” whichever is higher вЂ” for failures in incident detection and reporting.

The Scale of the Problem

AI-specific attacks increased 67% year-over-year. Today, 2,500+ distinct AI threats are observed daily. The average cost of an AI-related breach has reached $4.2 million вЂ” and that figure is growing 45% annually.

These are not theoretical risks. They are active threat vectors running through the same LLM infrastructure your teams deployed to meet product deadlines.

The Governance Gap Traditional DLP Doesn't Close

Traditional DLP was built for file transfers and email. It was not built for conversational AI traffic вЂ” the prompt layer where sensitive data now moves at volume. Research shows 8.5% of prompts contain sensitive data, yet 89% of enterprises have no purpose-built controls for AI-specific data flows.

Traditional DLP also does not address the EU AI Act's Article 10 data governance requirements, nor does it generate the audit trails NIS2 incident reporting demands for AI systems.

Shadow AI Compounds Every Risk in Scope

98% of employees use unsanctioned AI tools. Among the organisations that know about it, 75% have no governance framework covering those tools. Each unsanctioned deployment is an undocumented data processor вЂ” one your DPO cannot map and your SOC cannot monitor.

For the 160,000+ entities now in scope under NIS2, that gap is not an operational inconvenience. It is a direct compliance exposure with a defined penalty ceiling.

What the Regulatory Frame Requires Next

NIS2 mandates active incident detection, structured reporting timelines, and demonstrable technical controls. The EU AI Act adds data governance obligations at the model-input level. These two frameworks now overlap directly with every LLM integration in your production environment.

68% of European businesses report they struggle to understand their EU AI Act responsibilities. That uncertainty does not delay enforcement dates.

Over the next four emails, we'll map exactly where these exposures appear in a typical EU enterprise AI stack вЂ” and what controls address each layer under NIS2 and the EU AI Act.

Start with the threat picture. Download the AI threat reference to see what 27+ attack categories look like at the prompt layer.

Read the AI Threat Breakdown

APIRE  В·  [Physical address placeholder]  В·  EU

sequence.subject: 86% of companies had an AI security incident last year. Most didn't know until after.
sequence.previewText: The numbers behind Europe's AI security exposure вЂ” before we talk about solutions.
sequence.htmlBody:
APIRE

The exposure was already there.
The data just makes it harder to look away.

Before any conversation about solutions, the threat picture deserves a clear read. The statistics coming out of AI security research in 2025 describe a structural gap вЂ” not an edge case вЂ” in how European enterprises are running generative AI today.

The Incident Rate

86% of companies experienced an AI security incident last year.

That figure is not a projection. It is a reported rate across organisations that have already deployed AI in production. At the same time, AI-specific attacks are growing at 67% year-on-year, with 2,500+ distinct threats observed daily.

The volume is not the only problem. The character of the threat has changed. Prompt injection, data exfiltration through model outputs, and indirect attacks via compromised data sources are not detectable by signature-based or traditional DLP tooling вЂ” which was built for file transfers and email, not conversational AI traffic.

What a Breach Costs

$4.2M

Average cost of an AI-related breach

  

+$670K

Additional exposure when shadow AI is involved

Breach costs in AI environments are rising at 45% annually. The shadow AI variable is particularly relevant: 98% of employees use unsanctioned AI tools, and 75% of organisations using shadow AI have no governance framework in place to control what data those tools are processing.

Under NIS2, those same breaches вЂ” if they affect essential or important entities вЂ” carry fines of up to в‚¬10 million or 2% of global turnover, whichever is higher. Financial exposure from a single uncontrolled AI incident can run across both columns simultaneously.

The Governance Gap

90% of organisations are currently implementing or planning LLM use cases. 89% of those enterprises have no purpose-built AI security controls in place. That gap вЂ” between deployment velocity and security posture вЂ” is where the 2,500+ daily threats find their opening.

68%

of European businesses struggle to understand EU AI Act responsibilities

  

40%

of EU IT spending already goes toward compliance costs

  

8.5%

of prompts entering AI systems contain sensitive data

Why Traditional DLP Doesn't Close This

Traditional DLP was built for structured data movement across known channels. It does not inspect conversational AI traffic. It does not address NIS2 incident reporting requirements for AI systems. It has no framework for the EU AI Act.

The 160,000+ EU entities inside NIS2's scope are being asked to demonstrate active security controls and audit-ready incident reporting across their AI stack вЂ” using tooling that was never designed to see that traffic at all.

In the next email, we'll map exactly which points in your AI request pipeline these threats enter вЂ” and what controls belong at each layer.

If you'd rather read the full threat landscape now, the AI Security Maturity Model assessment walks through all 27+ threat categories with a gap analysis specific to your architecture.

Download the Maturity Assessment

+$670,000 вЂ” Additional exposure when shadow AI is involved

Breach costs in AI environments are rising at 45% annually. The shadow AI variable is particularly relevant: 98% of employees use unsanctioned AI tools, and 75% of organisations using shadow AI have no governance framework in place to control what data those tools are processing.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

THE GOVERNANCE GAP
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

90% of organisations are currently implementing or planning LLM use cases. 89% of those enterprises have no purpose-built AI security controls in place. That gap вЂ” between deployment velocity and security posture вЂ” is where the 2,500+ daily threats find their opening.

Key figures:
- 68% of European businesses struggle to understand EU AI Act responsibilities
- 40% of EU IT spending already goes toward compliance costs
- 8.5% of prompts entering AI systems contain sensitive data

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

WHY TRADITIONAL DLP DOESN'T CLOSE THIS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Traditional DLP was built for structured data movement across known channels. It does not inspect conversational AI traffic. It does not address NIS2 incident reporting requirements for AI systems. It has no framework for the EU AI Act.

The 160,000+ EU entities inside NIS2's scope are being asked to demonstrate active security controls and audit-ready incident reporting across their AI stack вЂ” using tooling that was never designed to see that traffic at all.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

WHAT'S NEXT
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

sequence.subject: The gap traditional DLP can't close вЂ” and what fills it
sequence.previewText: AI conversational traffic is a different attack surface. Standard controls weren't built for it.
sequence.htmlBody:
APIRE
 AI Security

Traditional DLP sees files and endpoints.
It does not see what your AI is being asked.

AI conversational traffic вЂ” the prompts entering your LLM stack and the completions leaving it вЂ” is a distinct attack surface that predates none of your existing controls. The category of protection this surface demands is not an extension of DLP, endpoint security, or WAF. It is a purpose-built discipline: inline governance of AI-layer traffic, in real time, before data moves.

Why existing controls fall short

Traditional DLP was designed for structured data at rest or in motion across known channels вЂ” email, cloud storage, endpoints. It was not built to parse the semantic content of a conversational prompt, detect a prompt injection attack embedded in a document your AI is summarising, or produce the audit trail NIS2 incident reporting requires for AI systems. The EU AI Act adds a further layer: obligations on data governance, human oversight, and transparency that DLP frameworks do not address.

The surface area DLP leaves exposed

Consider what travels through your AI layer every day. 8.5% of prompts submitted to enterprise AI systems contain sensitive data. 98% of employees use unsanctioned AI tools at some point. Each exchange is a potential exfiltration path, a vector for prompt injection, or a compliance event requiring documentation. None of those interactions appear in a DLP log. None are inspected at the point of transmission to an LLM. And under NIS2, the 160,000+ entities in scope cannot report incidents they never detected.

What this category of control must do

Effective AI-layer governance operates inline вЂ” sitting between your applications and AI providers, inspecting every prompt and completion before data moves. It must block threats in real time across a threat surface that includes prompt injection, sensitive data exfiltration, and model manipulation. It must apply policy without storing the data it processes, so that the control layer itself does not become a compliance liability. And it must produce audit-ready records that satisfy NIS2 incident reporting requirements вЂ” not as a post-hoc export, but as a continuous output of normal operation.

The scale of the gap

89% of enterprises lack purpose-built AI security controls.
A $4.2M average AI breach cost вЂ” $670,000 higher when shadow AI is involved.
AI-specific attacks increased 67% year-on-year. The attack surface is not static.
NIS2 fines reach в‚¬10 million or 2% of global turnover вЂ” whichever is higher вЂ” for entities that cannot demonstrate adequate controls.

In our next email we'll show you exactly what a purpose-built AI security platform does at each layer вЂ” and how it maps to the specific NIS2 and EU AI Act obligations your team is accountable for. Before then, the question worth answering internally: do you currently have a log of every sensitive data item that entered your LLM stack in the last 30 days?

Assess your AI security maturity

APIRE В· [Physical address placeholder] В· EU

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Traditional DLP was designed for structured data at rest or in motion across known channels вЂ” email, cloud storage, endpoints. It was not built to parse the semantic content of a conversational prompt, detect a prompt injection attack embedded in a document your AI is summarising, or produce the audit trail NIS2 incident reporting requires for AI systems. The EU AI Act adds a further layer: obligations on data governance, human oversight, and transparency that DLP frameworks do not address.


THE SURFACE AREA DLP LEAVES EXPOSED
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
8.5% of prompts submitted to enterprise AI systems contain sensitive data. 98% of employees use unsanctioned AI tools at some point. Each exchange is a potential exfiltration path, a vector for prompt injection, or a compliance event requiring documentation. None of those interactions appear in a DLP log. None are inspected at the point of transmission to an LLM. And under NIS2, the 160,000+ entities in scope cannot report incidents they never detected.


WHAT THIS CATEGORY OF CONTROL MUST DO
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Effective AI-layer governance operates inline вЂ” sitting between your applications and AI providers, inspecting every prompt and completion before data moves. It must block threats in real time across a threat surface that includes prompt injection, sensitive data exfiltration, and model manipulation. It must apply policy without storing the data it processes, so that the control layer itself does not become a compliance liability. And it must produce audit-ready records that satisfy NIS2 incident reporting requirements вЂ” not as a post-hoc export, but as a continuous output of normal operation.


THE SCALE OF THE GAP
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
- 89% of enterprises lack purpose-built AI security controls.
- A $4.2M average AI breach cost вЂ” $670,000 higher when shadow AI is involved.
- AI-specific attacks increased 67% year-on-year.
- NIS2 fines reach в‚¬10 million or 2% of global turnover вЂ” whichever is higher вЂ” for entities that cannot demonstrate adequate controls.


In our next email we'll show you exactly what a purpose-built AI security platform does at each layer вЂ” and how it maps to the specific NIS2 and EU AI Act obligations your team is accountable for. Before then, the question worth answering internally: do you currently have a log of every sensitive data item that entered your LLM stack in the last 30 days?


в†’ Assess your AI security maturity: https://apire.io/ai-security-maturity


в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
APIRE В· [Physical address placeholder] В· EU

sequence.subject: APIRE named. Here's what makes it different from everything else you've evaluated.
sequence.previewText: Purpose-built for AI traffic. Zero-Retention Architecture. 5-minute deploy. Here's the full picture.
sequence.htmlBody:
APIRE
AI Security Without Friction

You've seen the alternatives.
Here's why CISOs across the EU choose APIRE instead.

Traditional DLP was built for files and email вЂ” not for conversational AI traffic carrying PII, PHI, and proprietary data across multiple LLM providers simultaneously. APIRE was built from the ground up for exactly that environment, with NIS2 and the EU AI Act as design constraints, not afterthoughts.

Zero-Retention Architecture вЂ” not a feature. A design principle.

Every other layer in your AI stack stores something вЂ” logs, caches, request histories. APIRE's Zero-Retention Architecture (ZRA) means no personal data is written to logs, databases, or backups at the platform layer. No stored data means no primary object for a GDPR breach notification. No data residency trigger from the middleware itself. No PHI persisting where it has no business being.

When 8.5% of prompts sent to AI systems contain sensitive data вЂ” and your organisation runs AI at scale вЂ” the math on stored-log risk is not abstract.

Three gaps the alternatives leave open

1

Traditional DLP doesn't inspect AI conversational traffic.

It was designed for file transfers and email. Prompt injection, data exfiltration through model outputs, and indirect attacks via compromised data sources are architecturally invisible to it. APIRE's 5-layer defense system addresses 27+ distinct AI-specific threat categories in real time.
2

Generic AI security tools don't map to NIS2 incident reporting or EU AI Act articles.

APIRE provides audit-ready dashboards built to support NIS2 incident reporting obligations вЂ” and maps directly to EU AI Act Articles 10, 14, 15, and 52. For the 160,000+ entities now in scope under NIS2, generic tooling doesn't close that reporting gap.
3

Shadow AI governance requires coverage at the traffic layer, not the policy document layer.

98% of employees use unsanctioned AI. Policies in a wiki don't intercept a prompt. APIRE deploys in under 5 minutes, zero code changes, and begins enforcing at the API layer immediately вЂ” across every LLM provider your organisation uses, including the ones you haven't formally approved yet.

The cost of the status quo is specific

Organisations with shadow AI face breach costs running $670,000 higher on average. The average AI breach already costs $4.2M. NIS2 non-compliance exposes you to fines of up to в‚¬10M or 2% of global turnover вЂ” whichever is higher. And AI-specific attacks are increasing 67% year-over-year, with 2,500+ threats observed daily.

APIRE's 950+ pre-configured data protection rules, real-time inline masking, and closed models вЂ” never trained on customer data вЂ” are the operational answer to those numbers, not a conceptual one.

Next step

The fifth and final email in this sequence covers deployment and what the first 30 days look like in practice. If you'd rather move faster, a 30-minute technical walkthrough covers the full 5-layer architecture against your specific environment.

Request a technical walkthrough

No sales deck. No generic demo. We walk through your AI stack.

APIRE вЂ” AI Security Without Friction

[COMPANY PHYSICAL ADDRESS PLACEHOLDER]

You're receiving this because you expressed interest in APIRE's NIS2 and AI security resources.
Unsubscribe  |  Manage preferences

98% of employees use unsanctioned AI. Policies in a wiki don't intercept a prompt. APIRE deploys in under 5 minutes, zero code changes, and begins enforcing at the API layer immediately вЂ” across every LLM provider your organisation uses, including the ones you haven't formally approved yet.


THE COST OF THE STATUS QUO IS SPECIFIC
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Organisations with shadow AI face breach costs running $670,000 higher on average. The average AI breach already costs $4.2M. NIS2 non-compliance exposes you to fines of up to в‚¬10M or 2% of global turnover вЂ” whichever is higher. And AI-specific attacks are increasing 67% year-over-year, with 2,500+ threats observed daily.

APIRE's 950+ pre-configured data protection rules, real-time inline masking, and closed models вЂ” never trained on customer data вЂ” are the operational answer to those numbers.


NEXT STEP
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

sequence.subject: What EU security teams say after deploying APIRE
sequence.previewText: Real results from teams protecting AI under NIS2. One step left.
sequence.htmlBody:
APIRE
AI Security Without Friction

Email 5 of 5 В· Day 21

What happens in the first 30 days after deployment

Over the past four emails we covered the regulatory exposure, the gap traditional DLP cannot close, and what makes APIRE architecturally different. This one is shorter: it is what EU security teams report once APIRE is running вЂ” and a single next step.

What the numbers look like post-deployment

8.5%

of all prompts entering enterprise AI systems contain sensitive data. APIRE's 5-layer defense system intercepts and masks that data inline вЂ” before it reaches the model вЂ” with zero code changes to the application.

2,500+

AI-specific threats observed daily across the platform. Security teams get audit-ready dashboards from day one вЂ” the kind of documented incident trail NIS2 incident reporting requires.

$670,000

higher average breach cost when shadow AI is present. 98% of employees already use unsanctioned AI tools. APIRE gives security teams visibility and control over that traffic вЂ” not a policy document, real-time blocking.

What security architects tell us after the first month

The feedback we hear most consistently from EU enterprise security teams falls into three categories:

1

NIS2 reporting stops being a fire drill.

Every detection event is logged with a human-readable audit trail. When an incident occurs, the documentation regulators require under NIS2 already exists. For the 160,000+ EU entities now in scope, that shift in posture matters.
2

The deployment takes 5 minutes. The questions from auditors stop.

Zero-Retention Architecture means no personal data persists inside APIRE вЂ” which removes the most common friction point in GDPR and EU AI Act audit conversations. There is nothing stored to audit.
3

The threat surface becomes visible for the first time.

89% of enterprises have no purpose-built AI security in place. Once APIRE is running, teams see prompt injection attempts, data exfiltration patterns, and policy violations across every connected model вЂ” 27+ threat categories, in real time.

One step remaining

This is email five. If you have read this far, you have a specific problem вЂ” AI systems in production or rolling out, NIS2 obligations that attach to them, and a DLP stack that was not built to address either.

The step we ask for is a focused 30-minute technical session with our security architects. No product tour, no general pitch. We map your AI deployment against the 27+ threat categories APIRE covers, identify where your current controls have gaps, and show you exactly what a deployment looks like in your environment.

If it is not the right fit, you will know that in 30 minutes too. Fines up to в‚¬10 million or 2% of global turnover focus the conversation.

Book a 30-Minute Technical Session

No slides. No demo script. Your environment, your threat model.

You are receiving this because you opted into security communications from APIRE.
Unsubscribe  В·  Manage preferences

APIRE В· [Company Physical Address Placeholder] В· EU
